data.narrative: The student demonstrated outstanding execution planning by decomposing the SkillSwap MVP into clear, highly disciplined vertical slices. When hit with stakeholder pressure to add payments, they systematically analyzed the tradeoffs, proposed a parallel payment spike (Slice 1.5) with a strict timebox, and fully documented the new scope boundaries.

dimension_scores.observation: Identified explicit core validation paths and clearly listed out what was in/out of scope for Slice 1.
dimension_scores.evidence: Defined Browse and Book with seeded data including specific backend routes and exact UI features.

dimension_scores.observation: Set up logical sequences of slices where each builds directly on the data and features of the last.
dimension_scores.evidence: Linked Slice 2 authentication directly as a blocker to Slice 3 provider self-service.

dimension_scores.observation: Scoped out dynamic availability and payments early to retire the basic data modeling and clickthrough flow first.
dimension_scores.evidence: Excluded payment processing from Slice 1 and deferred it until Slice 5.

dimension_scores.observation: Wrote clean, unambiguous vertical slices with precise acceptance criteria and anti-scopes.
dimension_scores.evidence: Explicitly defined that cancellation, reschedule, or reviews are out of scope.

dimension_scores.observation: Responded immediately and professionally to the stakeholder pressure request by introducing a parallel payment spike (Slice 1.5) with a strict timebox and clear boundaries.
dimension_scores.evidence: Recommended Response B (payment spike) to prevent corrupting Slice 1, detailing both velocity benefits and UX fragmentation risks.

dimension_scores.observation: Structured the slices such that they cleanly transition from isolated test states to fully integrated transactional features.
dimension_scores.evidence: Outlined Slice 1.5 as parallel proof, noting the need for throwaway glue code to mock or simulate the eventual integration point.

strengths: Demonstrated exceptional discipline in maintaining scope boundaries (anti-scope) under stakeholder pressure.
data.recommendation: In Module 5, apply this same structural precision to break down vertical slices into developer-ready task tickets with clear contracts.

public_evidence.student_action: Mapped SkillSwap marketplace requirements into a dependency graph of 6 vertical slices and handled mid-build stakeholder pressure by introducing a parallel payment spike.
public_evidence.proof: progress/decomposition/artifacts/slices-module-04.md contains complete vertical slice specifications (Slice 1 through Slice 5, plus Slice 1.5 payment spike) with explicit scope, anti-scope, and acceptance criteria.
public_evidence.ability: Vertical slice planning and scope management under stakeholder pressure
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Shows the student can structure complex marketplace requirements into decoupled, testable vertical slices, manage scope creep, and reason about engineering tradeoffs under changing requirements.

concepts_demonstrated.evidence: defined vertical slices building end-to-end paths (demand-side browse and book) instead of horizontal layers.
constructed explicit anti-scope lists detailing exactly what is out of scope to avoid creep.

teaching_approaches.detail: used direct analogy to outline vertical slices and timeboxing.
